title: Slack login recovery reference
url: https://benchmark.local/slack/login-recovery-reference

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Sign in to Slack | Slack
- url:\
  \ https://slack.com/help/articles/212681477-Sign-in-to-Slack
- source: Slack Help\
  \ Center
- domain: slack.com

## Content

# Sign in to Slack

Slack explains\
  \ that members can use the same email address to sign in to one or more Slack workspaces.\
  \ The page describes sign-in with email, Apple, or Google, and includes troubleshooting\
  \ steps for common sign-in problems.

## Sign in to your workspace

### Sign\
  \ in with email

1. Open the Slack desktop app and choose Sign In to Slack, or\
  \ visit slack.com/signin from a browser.
2. Enter your email address and choose\
  \ the email sign-in option.
3. Check your email for a confirmation code from Slack\
  \ and enter that code.
4. If single sign-on is required for the workspace or organization,\
  \ complete authentication with the company’s SSO provider to finish signing\
  \ in.

### Sign in with Apple or Google

1. Open the Slack desktop app and choose\
  \ Sign In to Slack, or visit slack.com/signin.
2. Choose Continue with Apple or\
  \ Continue with Google and select the relevant account.
3. If SSO is required for\
  \ the workspace or organization, authenticate with the company’s SSO provider\
  \ to complete sign-in.

If a member belongs to more than one workspace, Slack\
  \ may show an option to select the workspace to open.

## Sign in to additional\
  \ workspaces

If you are already signed in to one workspace and need another,\
  \ Slack directs desktop users to the workspace icon in the sidebar, then Add a workspace,\
  \ then Sign in to another workspace. Mobile users can add another workspace from\
  \ the Home tab by selecting Add a Workspace and following the prompts.

## Troubleshoot\
  \ issues with signing in

Slack lists several common sign-in issues:

- If you\
  \ forgot your password, use Slack’s password-free sign-in to request a code\
  \ for workspaces associated with your email address. After you sign back in, you\
  \ can set a new password.
- If you are not receiving emails, check that you are\
  \ using the correct Slack account email. Check spam or junk folders. If Slack emails\
  \ are not delivered, Slack recommends asking your IT team to allow email from slack.com\
  \ and slack-mail.com.
- If you did not receive a 2FA code, review two-factor authentication\
  \ troubleshooting. Slack says you may need to ask a Workspace Owner or Admin to\
  \ temporarily disable 2FA for your account so you can sign in.
- If you created\
  \ a Slack account through Apple with a hidden relay address and need a different\
  \ email address, Slack describes options involving account settings or creating\
  \ a new account with a visible email address.

Slack also provides a support contact\
  \ path for people who are still having trouble.
"
metadata: {}
title: Sign in to Slack | Slack
url: https://slack.com/help/articles/212681477-Sign-in-to-Slack

Your workspace is active, but access is blocked because **Northstar Analytics enforces SSO** and the current sign-in method doesn’t match the workspace’s allowed method. You’re also a regular member without admin-console permissions, so you can’t fix the workspace authentication settings yourself.

### What to do next

1. Go to **https://slack.com/signin**.
2. Enter your Northstar work email: **[your usual `@northstar.example` address]**.
3. Choose the option to continue through your company’s **SSO/identity provider**—not Google, Apple, or a standalone Slack password.
4. Complete your company MFA prompt.

If you still receive `LOGIN_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED`:

- Contact your company’s **IT/help desk or Slack Workspace Owner/Admin** and report:
  - Workspace: **Northstar Analytics**
  - Error: **LOGIN_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED**
  - Issue: **authentication method mismatch**
  - Workspace setting: **SSO enforced**
- Ask them to verify that your account is assigned to the Northstar Slack application in the identity provider and that your email matches your Slack membership.
- If you recently changed email addresses, changed identity-provider accounts, or were re-provisioned, ask IT to check your **SCIM provisioning** record.

Do not try to reset a Slack password as the primary fix: with SSO enforced, password recovery generally won’t resolve this mismatch. Slack’s sign-in guidance also recommends checking spam/junk and asking IT to allow mail from `slack.com` and `slack-mail.com` if you aren’t receiving sign-in messages.

final_answer:
Your workspace is active, but access is blocked because **Northstar Analytics enforces SSO** and the current sign-in method doesn’t match the workspace’s allowed method. You’re also a regular member without admin-console permissions, so you can’t fix the workspace authentication settings yourself.

### What to do next

1. Go to **https://slack.com/signin**.
2. Enter your Northstar work email: **[your usual `@northstar.example` address]**.
3. Choose the option to continue through your company’s **SSO/identity provider**—not Google, Apple, or a standalone Slack password.
4. Complete your company MFA prompt.

If you still receive `LOGIN_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED`:

- Contact your company’s **IT/help desk or Slack Workspace Owner/Admin** and report:
  - Workspace: **Northstar Analytics**
  - Error: **LOGIN_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED**
  - Issue: **authentication method mismatch**
  - Workspace setting: **SSO enforced**
- Ask them to verify that your account is assigned to the Northstar Slack application in the identity provider and that your email matches your Slack membership.
- If you recently changed email addresses, changed identity-provider accounts, or were re-provisioned, ask IT to check your **SCIM provisioning** record.

Do not try to reset a Slack password as the primary fix: with SSO enforced, password recovery generally won’t resolve this mismatch. Slack’s sign-in guidance also recommends checking spam/junk and asking IT to allow mail from `slack.com` and `slack-mail.com` if you aren’t receiving sign-in messages.
